title: Gold-Silver Commodities — RSM Structural Analysis and Prediction Test

data_sources.gold_multi_currency: World Gold Council, 19 currencies, monthly since 1978 (245 months with full coverage)

carrier_a_returns_19_currency.description: Monthly gold returns in 19 currencies, softmax-normalized to simplex

carrier_c_returns_6_major.description: Monthly gold returns in 6 major currencies (USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, INR, CNY), softmax to simplex

prediction_verdict.can_rsm_predict_ratio_transitions: NO — not from currency structure alone
evidence: Forward regression R² is negative or near-zero at all lead times
prediction_verdict.why: Gold's cross-currency return structure captures FX dynamics (which currencies gold appreciates in fastest). The gold/silver ratio is driven by different forces: industrial demand, monetary policy, speculative flows. These are structurally orthogonal — similar to Backblaze where K_eff doesn't predict failure rate (r=-0.06).
prediction_verdict.analogy: This is a T5-type finding: the currency-structure carrier and the gold/silver ratio are in different information domains. The carrier correctly measures something (gold's FX structure), but that something doesn't predict the ratio.

what_rsm_CAN_do_for_gold_silver.detection_not_prediction: RSM can DETECT major structural transitions in the gold/silver ratio after they begin (z-score regime detection), but cannot PREDICT them from currency structure before they happen.

regime_detection.method: 12-month rolling z-score on ratio time series

below_50.interpretation: When ratio falls below 50 (silver very expensive relative to gold), historical tendency is for ratio to revert upward

above_80.interpretation: When ratio exceeds 80 (gold very expensive relative to silver), historical tendency is for ratio to revert downward

current_state_march_2026.interpretation: At 61, the ratio is at the 42th percentile of post-1978 history. This is NORMAL territory.

cl03_status_update.prior_status: ADD* — Hard gate not passed with real-data K_eff
cl03_status_update.this_test: Gold/silver cross-currency carrier does NOT predict ratio transitions. CL-03 remains ADD*.
cl03_status_update.finding: Prediction requires a carrier whose elements are causally upstream of the target variable. Gold's currency structure is not causally upstream of the gold/silver ratio. This is a domain-selection issue, not a framework failure.
cl03_status_update.recommendation: For gold/silver prediction, the carrier would need to include industrial demand indicators, monetary policy signals, speculative positioning — variables that are causally connected to relative precious metal prices. RSM with a currency-only carrier correctly reports: structure and target are orthogonal.

rack_impact: No rack change. This is an exploratory application, not a calibration test. However, the T5-analog finding (structure-target orthogonality in commodities) strengthens the evidence that RSM correctly identifies information boundaries.
